promoted_facts.fact: Marketing income comprises ~41% of total franchisor revenue (FY2024 ~$828K of $2,027,743)
Advertising expense $285,153 vs marketing income ~$828,000 → ~$543K net retained / surplus from marketing fund (consistent with 40% admin allocation in Item 11)
PTET payments $79,245 (2024) and $42,132 (2023) are included in distributions for accounting purposes — meaning the headline distribution figure overstates pure profit-extraction by ~$79K

Initial franchise fee revenue recognition: ~50% at franchise opening + completion of training; ~50% straight-line over 10-year contract life. If location closes early, remaining unearned revenue is recognized into income at closure.
Useful life: 3 years for both website development and furniture/fixtures
Customer concentration: majority of accounts receivable from one customer in both FY2024 and FY2023
Subsequent events evaluated through 2025-03-24 — no events disclosed
Deferred franchise fees grew 93.8% YoY (from $309,063 to $598,854) — strong forward-bookings signal
Two of three master franchisees are also Cupbop directors: Kevin Santiago (Gold Bowl LLC, ~26 units) and Yong Min Kim (Cupbop Oklahoma, 3 units)
promoted_facts.source: Item 2 + Exhibit F franchisee list, pages 10 + 167–168

promoted_facts.fact: Cupbop Co. concessions at Vivint Smart Home Arena, Rice-Eccles Stadium, and LaVell Edwards Stadium are NOT counted in Item 20 outlet tables
promoted_facts.source: Item 20 Notes to Tables Note 2, page 47

promoted_facts.fact: 0 credit losses and 0 impairments recorded in FY2024 and FY2023
promoted_facts.source: Note 1 A/R policy + Long-Lived Asset Impairment, pages 62–63

promoted_facts.fact: Auditor consent dated 2025-03-26 references both 2025-03-24 and 2024-05-31 audit reports
FA Guaranty (Exhibit H) is unconditional, joint-and-several, primary and independent of franchisee liability, with explicit waiver of statute of limitations and right to require pursuit of others
